blockingIssues.description: The reviewed backend commit published the beacon machine name from the generic computer-name source instead of the dedicated Sync Beacon machine-name setting, so custom beacon names were not actually used until a later fix.

Sync Beacon payload generation reads last-commit metadata from the ProjectsContext status cache, but that cache does not include lastCommitHash, lastCommitMessage, or lastCommitTimestamp, so remote beacon entries can still publish blank commit details.
The follow-up fix corrected backend machine naming but still left the frontend beacon payload wired to the wrong status store, so last-commit metadata remains empty in published beacon data.

The shared GitStatus type was not updated to model lastCommitHash, lastCommitMessage, and lastCommitTimestamp even though the daemon now returns them, leaving the intended metadata path untyped and unused.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the tauri-fullstack skill to explicitly cover validation strategies for backend commands that shell out to external CLIs such as `gh`, including how to exercise create/update/error branches and config persistence without relying only on helper-level unit tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The sync-beacon-backend review found that the gh-backed command surface was shipped with only helper/JSON tests and missed the wrong machine-name source, requiring a later fix feature.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in tauri-fullstack that browser/dev-server verification is only a limited smoke check for Tauri-dependent features, and document when workers should instead rely on Tauri/runtime-aware validation paths for features like Sync Beacon.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The sync-beacon-frontend review noted that browser-only checks could confirm navigation/layout but not the Tauri + gh-backed beacon behavior, yet the current skill still frames browser verification as the default manual step.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that ProjectsContext status polling does not include last-commit metadata, and that Sync Beacon payloads must source commit details from the full daemon git-status path instead of the lightweight project-status cache.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both the sync-beacon-frontend review and the follow-up fix review found the same bug pattern: beacon payload generation assumed commit metadata existed in `projectsState.statuses`, which caused blank last-commit fields in published beacon data.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the distinction between the generic `computerName`/hostname field and the dedicated `syncBeaconMachineName` setting so future workers do not wire beacon identity to the wrong config source.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The sync-beacon-backend review found the original backend implementation used the generic computer name rather than the Sync Beacon machine-name setting, and the later fix feature existed solely to repair that mismatch.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a skill note that plumbing-only fixes may reasonably skip browser verification and strict Rust-first TDD in some cases.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because this came from a single follow-up fix review and is weaker than the broader, higher-signal guidance to distinguish browser smoke checks from Tauri-runtime validation for this milestone.
